The fall schedule begins Sunday, August 24th. Returning students will be contacted soon — here's what you need to know about scheduling and monthly billing.

We are starting work on the fall schedule, which begins **Sunday, August 24**! Check your emails for more details, but here's the short version:

- Returning private students will get an email/call from their teacher during the week of August 7th. We will do our best to let students retain their lesson time from last year if desired.
- Returning hour-long group students will be contacted beginning August 1.
- We will schedule new students & the returning K–1st group students beginning August 11.

With the return to the school year we also return to monthly billing. Students receive 34 lessons during the school year and that cost is distributed evenly so students are charged the same every month. There's no additional fee for practice recitals and recitals.

Eric will be working on the schedule every day so don't hesitate to contact him.

Enjoy the rest of the summer!

Lessons resume the week of January 5th. All lesson times remain the same as December. Spring recitals are set for May 18–21 at the Lawrence Arts Center.

Piano lessons for all students in group and private lessons will begin again the week of **January 5, 2026**.

All lesson times will remain the same as December unless we have discussed a change. Please let us know ASAP if you need to change your lesson time. We will be making adjustments to the schedule as well as scheduling new students before we begin January 5th.

## Group Studio Recitals

All students in **hour-long group lessons** will have a **studio recital the week of March 30th**, two weeks after Lawrence Public Schools' spring break. This recital will be at the studio during your regular lesson time.

## Spring Recitals

We will be having our Spring Recitals at the **Lawrence Arts Center** for all students **(both group and private).**

These recitals are scheduled for **Monday, May 18th – Thursday May 21st**.

Recital sign-up information will be sent at the beginning of April.

Have a great 2026!

— Eric

Laura King is now a business partner of Lawrence Piano Studio as of January 1st, 2026 — a milestone for the studio's future.

Laura King, who has been teaching at the studio since 2009, is my business partner as of January 1st.

This is not a notice of impending retirement but a strategy for moving forward. As I approach my 70th birthday in April, I realized I needed a plan for the future of the studio. I have been tapering my instructional time over the last few years and now I will be able to taper my administrative duties as well.

Laura will eventually be the sole owner of the studio; that will probably happen in the next 5–10 years. I still have too much fun interacting with students and teachers to give that up.

I look forward to seeing everyone at lessons in January.

— Eric

Studio recitals kick off this week, private student recitals are set for Dec 15–17 at the Arts Center, and the holiday break runs Dec 14 through Jan 3.

## Studio Recitals

Group studio recitals begin today. Students should arrive at their regularly scheduled lesson time to warm up with the class. Studio recitals will start 15 minutes after the normal lesson time. Families are encouraged to attend in-person, but Zoom invitations have also been emailed. We will have a brief reception following each recital.

The recital will be the last class before the holiday break. We will resume lessons the week of January 4th.

## Private Student Recitals

Students in private lessons will have a recital at the Arts Center, **December 15–17**. Friends and family are encouraged to attend in person; the Arts Center will live stream for those unable to attend. We will have a reception following each recital.

## Schedule Updates

During the holiday break, we will make some minor schedule adjustments. Some group students may be moved to different classes, to balance student numbers, adjust for difficulty, or resolve schedule conflicts. Private teachers may adjust their schedules as applicable. If you may be interested in changing classes, please email us.

## Piano Tuning

As the weather turns colder, your piano may be affected by the change in humidity. Now is a good time to get your piano tuned. There are many good piano tuners in Lawrence; let us know if you would like a contact number.

## Holiday Break

There are no lessons from **Sunday, December 14th through Saturday, January 3**. Lessons will resume Monday, January 5th.
